hero.subtitle: Rank for the searches that matter, strengthen site foundations, and publish content that compounds into traffic, enquiries, and revenue over time.

details.title: What We Do & Why It Matters
details.card1Desc: Many sites publish content without a search strategy and miss basic technical issues that suppress visibility. Rankings stagnate and organic traffic never becomes a growth channel.
details.card2Desc: We improve SEO from the ground up through technical audits, on-page optimisation, keyword planning, and link-worthy content strategies tied to commercial intent.
details.card3Desc: Stronger rankings for revenue-driving searches, a healthier website foundation, and organic growth that compounds rather than resetting every month.

subFeatures.title: Every Deliverable,
subFeatures.item1Desc: A full review of crawlability, indexing, speed, structure, and site issues that may be limiting search performance.
subFeatures.item2Desc: Title tags, headings, page structure, internal links, and content updates aligned with what users are actually searching for.
subFeatures.item3Desc: Content and outreach approaches designed to earn relevant links that strengthen topical authority and organic rankings.
subFeatures.item4Desc: Prioritisation of search themes across service, category, and content pages so each page has a clearer role in the overall strategy.
subFeatures.item5Desc: A plan for articles, landing pages, and supporting content that targets both demand capture and authority building.
subFeatures.item6Desc: Regular reporting on rankings, traffic, conversions, and priority opportunities so the work stays accountable to business outcomes.

caseStudy.title: Real Results for Real Businesses
caseStudy.case1Problem: The site had weak structure, poor page targeting, and low visibility on commercial queries. Organic traffic was underperforming despite strong service demand.
caseStudy.case2Problem: The business relied on paid traffic and branded searches, with little visibility for non-brand terms that new customers were actively using.
